rationale: X's top comment (reuse pipeline._dit_modules instead of a fourth hardcoded component list) closely mirrors dsocek's actual ground-truth concern about deriving the mapping from existing per-model metadata rather than hardcoding, and is backed with specific file/line citations and a concrete fix. Y approves with no comments and its own 'simplifiability' analysis explicitly dismisses extracting the list into a shared constant — the opposite conclusion from the real reviewer's suggestion — so it misses the one substantive concern in the ground truth entirely. Both sets of claims appear grounded in the diff/repo, but X is far more actionable (specific line-level suggestions) versus Y's approve-only output with an empty review_comments list.
